doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_abort_prec_max. The C function fprintfs to stderr and calls abort(); the TS port throws MPFRError('EPREC', 'Maximal precision overflow'). Status: DEFERRED from grading — the harness's wire codec cannot encode 'expected throw' cases (every thrown exception currently classifies as n_throw, the failure path). The port itself at src/ops/abort_prec_max.ts is correct; what's missing is harness support. This spec records the deferral so future passes don't reattempt the function before the harness extension lands.
divergence_from_c: C writes to stderr and calls abort() — process-terminating side effects unavailable in pure ESM TS (Rule 12). TS throws MPFRError('EPREC', ...) instead; abort() does not have a recoverable analog and a port that calls process.exit would (a) require node:process which violates Rule 12, and (b) corrupt the harness worker pool. The thrown exception is the only idiomatic translation.
blocked_by: harness wire codec does not support expected_throw test cases; extending runner.ts and value_codec.ts to accept {expected_throw: {code: string, message?: string}} is ~150-200 LOC of harness work and was deferred from this prep batch per the orchestrator's option (b) advisory.
refs: mpfr/src/abort_prec_max.c L24-L29 — the C reference. fprintf(stderr, ...) followed by abort().
src/ops/abort_prec_max.ts — the TS stub. Correct, but ungraded until harness supports expected_throw cases.
src/core.ts L197-L209 — MPFRError class. The 'EPREC' code is the closest semantic fit for a precision-domain overflow.
eval/harness/runner.ts §worker-protocol — currently classifies any thrown error as n_throw (the failure path); no expected_throw branch exists.
CLAUDE.md Rule 12 — no node:* / Bun.* imports in src/ (rules out process.exit as an abort() analog).
CLAUDE.md Rule 14 — phase / harness-schema changes go through worklog + ADR; the harness extension is one such change.
